Result: **Number of hotel nights:** **Estimated total cost of the stay:** ## How to use this calculator? 1.   Input your arrival date. 2.   Input your departure date. 3.   If desired, input the price for 1 night for the estimated total cost of the stay. 4.   Tick the \"Add 1 day\" box if you anticipate late check-out, and the hotel counts it as an additional night. ## How do hotels calculate nights? Hotel nights usually represent the nights you spend in a hotel. [Full Bio](https://calculator.academy/author/scott-hickam/) Enter the total length of stay, in nights, and", "relevance_score": 0.8}, {"title": "How to See the Real Prices for an Airbnb Listing (Fact-Based Guide)", "url": "https://lifetips.alibaba.com/tech-efficiency/how-to-see-the-real-prices-for-an-airbnb-listing", "snippet": "[Jina Extracted Content] Title: How to See the Real Prices for an Airbnb Listing (Fact-Based Guide) URL Source: https://lifetips.alibaba.com/tech-efficiency/how-to-see-the-real-prices-for-an-airbnb-listing Published Time: 2026-01-16 16:04:54 Markdown Content: True price transparency on Airbnb requires active intervention\u2014not passive browsing. The real price is **never displayed by default**. It is systematically obscured by layered, non-negotiable add-ons (cleaning fees, service fees, occupancy taxes) that only appear after selecting dates and clicking \u201cCheck availability.\u201d To see the actual per-night cost before commitment: (1) Open the listing in an incognito/private window with all extensions disabled; (2) Manually calculate base rate by dividing total pre-tax price (shown in final checkout preview) by number of nights\u2014 _not_ the \u201c$X/night\u201d headline; (3) Use browser DevTools (Ctrl+Shift+I \u2192 Elements tab) to locate the `data-price` attribute on the price container element, which reflects the unmarked base rate Airbnb uses internally; (4) Cross-verify using Airbnb\u2019s own API endpoint `/api/v3/pdp/price_quote` (accessible via curl or Postman with valid session token) to extract raw nightly base, cleaning, and service fee components. This method eliminates behavioral pricing bias, prevents location-based surcharges from inflating initial quotes, and reduces average price misestimation by 27.4% (per 2023 Stanford HCI Lab A/B test, n = 1,842 users). ## Why Airbnb\u2019s \u201cPer-Night\u201d Price Is a Cognitive Illusion Airbnb\u2019s interface design leverages well-documented principles of cognitive economics and attention residue. The prominent \u201c$129/night\u201d banner serves as a _reference anchor_\u2014a heuristic that biases subsequent judgments about value, even when users know it\u2019s incomplete. Eye-tracking studies (NN/g, 2022) confirm that 83% of users fixate first on this figure and disengage from the fine-print disclosure zone below the booking CTA. Crucially, this number excludes three mandatory, non-negotiable components: (1) Cleaning fee (typically $45\u2013$180, scaled by stay length but never shown per-night), (2) Airbnb service fee (12\u201316% of subtotal, calculated *after* cleaning fee), and (3) Occupancy tax (varies by jurisdiction, applied last). These are not optional upgrades\u2014they\u2019re contractual obligations baked into every reservation. This violates ISO 9241-210\u2019s human-centered design principle of \u201ctransparency of system status,\u201d because the UI deliberately withholds the most consequential financial variable\u2014the total cost per night inclusive of fixed overheads\u2014until the user has already invested cognitive effort in date selection and guest count entry. The result? A measurable increase in post-booking price shock: 41% of first-time Airbnb users report abandoning bookings at final checkout due to unexpected fees (Airbnb Trust & Safety Report, Q2 2023). That abandonment isn\u2019t frictionless\u2014it\u2019s costly friction, wasting ~2.8 minutes of user time per failed flow (measured via session replay ana", "relevance_score": 0.8}, {"title": "Hotel Rates & Pricing Data: Best Datasets & Databases 2026 | Datarade", "url": "https://datarade.ai/data-categories/hotel-rates-pricing-data", "snippet": "[Jina Extracted Content] Title: Just a moment...\nURL Source: https://datarade.ai/data-categories/hotel-rates-pricing-data\nWarning: Target URL returned error 403: Forbidden\nWarning: This page maybe requiring CAPTCHA, please make sure you are authorized to access this page.\nMarkdown Content:\n## datarade.ai\n## Performing security verification\nThis website uses a security service to protect against malicious bots. This page is displayed while the website verifies you are not a bot.", "relevance_score": 0.8}, {"title": "Understanding the Market insights reports", "url": "https://developers.booking.com/connectivity/docs/market-insights-api/understanding-the-market-insights-api", "snippet": "[Jina Extracted Content] Understanding property performance The Market insight API provides four endpoints that can provide insights into your property's performance.This section provides additional information on how to analyse the insights and understand the available metrics. ### [](https://developers.booking.com/connectivity/docs/market-insights-api/understanding-the-market-insights-api#analysing-the-metrics-impact) Analysing the metrics impact Using the property performance endpoints, you can compare the insights with data from: *   Last year: The API compares the report with the last year values, that is 365 days earlier than the date or time period when the report is run. You can see the percentage change. Useful for year-on-year comparison. *   A competitive set: A competitive set includes up to 50 properties selected by Booking.com based on what guests were looking at before booking a specific property. *   A peer group: A peer group consists of 50 similar properties in the target property's area. You can use the peer group to see how a specific property is doing compared to similar properties. The properties in the peer group are chosen by Booking.com based on similarities in conversion, cancellations, property type, and other factors. *   Market: All properties in a specific region. ### [](https://developers.booking.com/connectivity/docs/market-insights-api/understanding-the-market-insights-api#understanding-insight-terminologies) Understanding insight terminologies | Terminology | Description | | --- | --- | | Book date | The date a reservation is booked by guests, minus any cancellations. | | Stay date | A reservation\u2019s actual stay dates. Those are the dates guests will stay (or have already stayed) at a property, not the day they booked on Booking.com. | | Room night | The number of rooms booked or stayed on a given date or time period. For example, if a guest books a room for 3 nights, that\u2019s 3 room nights. But if the guest books two rooms for 3 nights, that\u2019s 6 room nights. | | ADR | Average daily rate calculated as the total revenue divided by room nights sold. | ## [](https://developers.booking.com/connectivity/docs/market-insights-api/understanding-the-market-insights-api#understanding-the-demand-data-report) Understanding the demand data report Using the `area_demand_data` endpoint, you can find: *   The guest's search window: The number of days ahead of their stay that guests took to search. *   The traveller type: For example, whether guests wanted to travel alone, as a couple, a family or a group. *   The most popular device type they use to search for the stay. For example, mobile or a computer. *   The cancellation policy attached to the stay. For example, you can find the details of cancellation policies that were most popular based on actual reservations. *   The top 5 countries from where the bulk of the guests' search come, based on IP address. *   The guests' stay destination, whether domestic or international. For example, guests from Franc", "relevance_score": 0.8}, {"title": "How to set up your base price? - PriceLabs Knowledge Base", "url": "https://help.pricelabs.co/portal/en/kb/articles/setting-base-price", "snippet": "[DDG Snippet] 3 weeks ago -Your base price is the average nightly rate you'd charge across the whole year- not your peak price, not your floor. It's the single number where PriceLabs applies the adjustments to account for demand, seasons, and market conditions to calculate ...", "relevance_score": 0.6}, {"title": "Market Dashboards - PriceLabs", "url": "https://hello.pricelabs.co/market-dashboards/", "snippet": "[DDG Snippet] 2 weeks ago -Data from individual sections can be exported as a CSV using the pink square in the upper right corner of each module. Note: underlying booking data cannot be exported. PriceLabs Dynamic Pricing is a market pricing tool that focuses on optimizing nightly rates in real time based on demand, seasonality, and local events.", "relevance_score": 0.6}, {"title": "Overnight \u2014 Indicators and Strategies \u2014 TradingView \u2014 India India", "url": "https://in.tradingview.com/scripts/overnight/", "snippet": "[DDG Snippet] The price of stocks generally behaves extremely differently during market hours and outside market hours, and many of the large moves happen outside of regular market hours on low volume. \u2699\ufe0f Core Features 1) OBV-style calculation: If price increases \u2192 volume is added to the OBV stream. If price decreases \u2192 volume is subtracted. If price is flat \u2192 OBV remains unchanged. 2) Session splitting: Intraday session: movement from today's open to close. Overnight session: movement from yesterday\u2019s close to today\u2019s open. Volume is split proportionally between these two periods based on user input. 3) Four visualization modes: \"Intraday\" \u2014 plots only OBV from intraday price movement.", "relevance_score": 0.6}]
